title: Docker layer caching for faster builds
titleAr: تخزين طبقات Docker المؤقت للبناء الأسرع
desc: Copy only dependency manifests first and install before copying the rest of the source. Docker reuses the cached layer when only code changes.
descAr: انسخ ملفات التبعيات أولاً ثم ثبتها قبل نسخ باقي المصدر. Docker يعيد استخدام الطبقة المخبأة عندما يتغير الكود فقط.
summary: Changing one line of source invalidated npm install, forcing a full re-download of dependencies every CI build. Reordering two COPY instructions drops build time from 3 minutes to 10 seconds.
problem: Every CI build copied the entire source, then ran npm install. Changing one line of source code invalidated the npm install cache layer, forcing a full re-download of dependencies every time. Builds took 3–5 minutes. Developers waste time waiting for builds, CI pipelines clog, and the feedback loop slows dramatically. The fix is reordering two lines in the Dockerfile.
problemTitle: Every source edit re-downloads all dependencies
solution: Reorder Dockerfile commands: 1. COPY package*.json ./ — copy manifests only 2. RUN npm ci --only=production — install deps 3. COPY . . — copy the rest of the source Docker caches each layer. If dependencies haven't changed, the npm install layer is reused and only the app code layer rebuilds. This drops build time from minutes to seconds.
solutionTitle: Copy manifests first, install, then copy source

callout.text: Key idea: Docker caches by layer, and a layer is invalidated when its contents or any previous layer changes. By putting volatile code after stable dependencies, the dependency cache survives source edits.

code.html: COPY package*.json ./ RUN npm ci --only=production COPY . .

baDesc: The change is two line swaps, but the build time difference is massive in CI.

before.desc: Any source change invalidates npm install.
before.code: FROM node:18 WORKDIR /app COPY . . // source first — invalidates everything RUN npm ci

after.code: FROM node:18 WORKDIR /app COPY package*.json ./ // manifests first RUN npm ci // cached unless package.json changes COPY . . // only this layer rebuilds
